message:
perf(vdbe): BTree-indexed unique constraint tracking for MemTable

MemTable::find_unique_conflicts previously performed a linear scan over
all rows for each UNIQUE column group — O(rows × constraints) per INSERT.
For tables with thousands of rows this became the dominant cost in
MemDatabase-mode workloads (parity tests, schema-only connections).

This commit adds a parallel BTreeMap<Vec<SqliteValue>, BTreeSet<i64>>
index for each registered UNIQUE column group. Key changes:

- add_unique_column_group: builds the BTreeMap index from existing rows
  at registration time
- find_unique_conflicts: O(log n) BTreeMap lookup per constraint instead
  of linear scan; returns BTreeSet-collected rowids for deterministic order
- insert: computes unique keys for new values, updates indexes on both
  fresh insert and UPSERT replace (removes old keys, adds new)
- delete_by_rowid: removes the deleted row's keys from all unique indexes
- clear: clears all indexes alongside the row vector
- pad_columns_to: triggers a full rebuild_unique_indexes after schema
  alteration to ensure index entries reflect the new column count

NULL handling preserved: unique_key_for_group returns None when any
participating column is NULL, matching SQLite's "NULL is distinct"
UNIQUE semantics. NULL keys are never inserted into indexes.

MemDbUndoOp paths updated to route through insert()/delete_by_rowid()
instead of directly manipulating the rows vector, ensuring undo
operations maintain index consistency.

Also adds row_count() and max_visible_rowid() helpers; the latter
replaces an iter().map().max() call in alloc_rowid_concurrent.

Tests:
- test_memdb_concurrent_rowid_uses_visible_max_not_stale_counter
- test_memtable_unique_conflict_index_tracks_insert_delete_and_update

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
